title: [InstCombine] Limit (icmp eq/ne (and (add A, Addend), Msk), C) fold to one use of and
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by recognizing that the pattern `(X + C1) & C2 != C3` can be transformed when `C3` falls within the range of possible `&` results. The key insight is that the comparison can be simplified to `X & C2 != (C3 - C1) & C2`, provided `(C3 - C1) & C2` doesn't overflow the mask's bitwidth.

In the original test, `(a + 10) & 15 != 11` becomes `a & 15 != 1`. The patch generalizes this by:
1. Matching the exact pattern `(X + Addend) & Mask != C`
2. Ensuring the mask operation is a low-bit mask (allowing direct bitwise reasoning)
3. Checking that the constant `C` is within the mask's range (`C.ule(Mask)`)
4. Computing the transformed constant as `(C - Addend) & Mask`

This transformation eliminates the addition when comparing against a masked value, reducing instruction count. The implementation uses precise matchers (`m_OneUse`, `m_LowBitMask`) to avoid correctness issues and applies only when the intermediate values have single uses, preventing unnecessary code duplication.

The strategy demonstrates how to elevate a specific case to a general algebraic simplification by identifying the underlying mathematical relationship and adding appropriate safety checks.
